evals.prompt: Benchmark the GPU RapidsUDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumRapidsUDF.scala against the original CPU UDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumUDF.scala, and report the speedup. A passing unit test is provided at input/array-segment-sum/UnitTest.scala.
evals.expected_output: Benchmarks the GPU RapidsUDF against the CPU UDF on synthetic data at scale and reports the CPU runtime, GPU runtime, and the resulting speedup.
assertions: Generates schema-compatible, representative variable-length synthetic data at scale
Runs both the CPU UDF and the GPU RapidsUDF on the same data
Reports the CPU runtime, GPU runtime, and the resulting speedup

evals.prompt: What does the spark.sql.shuffle.partitions setting control?
evals.expected_output: A one-line Spark-config answer; not a request to benchmark a UDF, so udf-benchmark should not activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-benchmark skill

evals.prompt: How do I read a Parquet file into a DataFrame in PySpark?
evals.expected_output: Basic Spark I/O question, unrelated to CPU-vs-GPU UDF benchmarking; no UDF skill should activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-benchmark skill
